5. `<InitgPty>` has only `<Nm>` (name) - no other identifiers
6. `<PmtInf>`:

   - `<PmtTpInf>`:

     - `<LclInstrm><Cd>CORE</Cd></LclInstrm>`
     - `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` - this is "Recurring" 
     - **Has `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`** - Category Purpose - this is a REQUIRED element per the SEPA spec for some configurations
   - `ReqdColltnDt`: `2026-05-04`
   - `<Cdtr><Nm>SV Eutingen 1947 e.V.</Nm></Cdtr>`
   - `<CdtrAcct><Id><IBAN>DE28642910100034469001</IBAN></Id><Ccy>EUR</Ccy></CdtrAcct>` - **Ccy is present**

   - **Has `<CdtrSchmeId>`** with `<PrvtId><Othr><Id>DE54ZZZ00000299406</Id><SchmeNm><Prtry>SEPA</Prtry></SchmeNm></Othr></PrvtId></Id>` - the creditor scheme identifier

1: use anyhow::{Result, anyhow, bail};
2: use chrono::Utc;
3: use iban::IbanLike;
4: use num_traits::ToPrimitive;
5: use quick_xml::Writer;
6: use quick_xml::events::Event as XmlEvent;
7: use quick_xml::events::{BytesDecl, BytesEnd, BytesStart, BytesText};
8: use tracing::warn;
9: use uuid::Uuid;
10: 
11: use crate::error::ValidationError;
12: use crate::models::{Event, EventSubscription};
13: 
14: pub(crate) fn validate_iban(raw: &str) -> Result<iban::Iban, ValidationError> {
15:     let normalized = raw.to_uppercase();
16:     normalized.parse().map_err(|_| {
17:         warn!("IBAN validation failed for input: {}", normalized);
18:         ValidationError::new("Bitte gib eine gültige IBAN ein.")
19:     })
20: }
21: 
22: pub(crate) fn validate_iban_str(raw: &str) -> Result<String, ValidationError> {
23:     Ok(validate_iban(raw)?.electronic_str().to_string())
24: }
25: 
26: pub(crate) async fn lookup_bic(iban: &str) -> Result<String> {
27:     let parsed = iban
28:         .parse::<iban::Iban>()
29:         .map_err(|_| anyhow!("Invalid IBAN: {}", iban))?;
30: 
31:     if let Some(bank_code) = parsed.bank_identifier()
32:         && let Some(bank) = fints_institute_db::get_bank_by_bank_code(bank_code)
33:     {
34:         return Ok(bank.bic.to_string());
35:     }
36: 
37:     let url = format!("https://bankcheck.dev/api/v1/validate?q={}", iban);
38:     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
39:     let response = client
40:         .get(&url)
41:         .send()
42:         .await
43:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("BIC lookup failed: {}", e))?;
44: 
45:     let json: serde_json::Value = response
46:         .json()
47:         .await
48:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to parse BIC response: {}", e))?;
49: 
50:     if let Some(bic) = json
51:         .get("result")
52:         .and_then(|r| r.get("bankInfo"))
53:         .and_then(|bi| bi.get("bic"))
54:         .and_then(|b| b.as_str())
55:         && !bic.is_empty()
56:     {
57:         return Ok(bic.to_string());
58:     }
59: 
60:     bail!("No BIC found for IBAN: {}", iban)
61: }
62: 
63: fn write_element(writer: &mut Writer<Vec<u8>>, name: &str, value: &str) -> Result<()> {
64:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new(name)))?;
65:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Text(BytesText::new(value)))?;
66:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new(name)))?;
67:     Ok(())
68: }
69: 
70: fn write_element_with_attr(
71:     writer: &mut Writer<Vec<u8>>,
72:     name: &str,
73:     attr: (&str, &str),
74:     text: &str,
75: ) -> Result<()> {
76:     let mut elem = BytesStart::new(name);
77:     elem.push_attribute(attr);
78:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(elem))?;
79:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Text(BytesText::new(text)))?;
80:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new(name)))?;
81:     Ok(())
82: }
83: 
84: pub(crate) fn generate_sepa_xml(
85:     event: &Event,
86:     bookings: &[(EventSubscription, String)],
87:     creditor_name: &str,
88:     creditor_iban: &str,
89:     creditor_bic: &str,
90: ) -> Result<String> {
91:     let mut writer = Writer::new_with_indent(Vec::new(), b' ', 2);
92: 
93:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Decl(BytesDecl::new("1.0", Some("UTF-8"), None)))?;
94: 
95:     let doc_attrs = vec![
96:         ("xmlns", "urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.008.001.02"),
97:         ("xmlns:xsi", "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"),
98:     ];
99:     let mut doc = BytesStart::new("Document");
100:     for (k, v) in &doc_attrs {
101:         doc.push_attribute((*k, *v));
102:     }
103:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(doc.clone()))?;
104: 
105:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("CstmrDrctDbtInitn")))?;
106: 
107:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("GrpHdr")))?;
108:     let msg_id = Uuid::new_v4().simple().to_string();
109:     write_element(&mut writer, "MsgId", &msg_id)?;
110:     write_element(
111:         &mut writer,
112:         "CreDtTm",
113:         &Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S").to_string(),
114:     )?;
115:     write_element(&mut writer, "NbOfTxs", &bookings.len().to_string())?;
116: 
117:     let ctrl_sum: f64 = bookings
118:         .iter()
119:         .map(|(sub, _)| sub.total_price(event).to_f64().unwrap_or(0.0))
120:         .sum();
121:     write_element(&mut writer, "CtrlSum", &format!("{:.2}", ctrl_sum))?;
122: 
123:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("InitgPty")))?;
124:     write_element(&mut writer, "Nm", creditor_name)?;
125:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("InitgPty")))?;
126:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("GrpHdr")))?;
127: 
128:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("PmtInf")))?;
129:     let pmt_inf_id = Uuid::new_v4().simple().to_string();
130:     write_element(&mut writer, "PmtInfId", &pmt_inf_id)?;
131:     write_element(&mut writer, "PmtMtd", "DD")?;
132:     write_element(&mut writer, "NbOfTxs", &bookings.len().to_string())?;
133:     write_element(&mut writer, "CtrlSum", &format!("{:.2}", ctrl_sum))?;
134: 
135:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("PmtTpInf")))?;
136:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("SvcLvl")))?;
137:     write_element(&mut writer, "Cd", "SEPA")?;
138:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("SvcLvl")))?;
139:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("LclInstrm")))?;
140:     write_element(&mut writer, "Cd", "CORE")?;
141:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("LclInstrm")))?;
142:     write_element(&mut writer, "SeqTp", "FRST")?;
143:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("PmtTpInf")))?;
144: 
145:     write_element(
146:         &mut writer,
147:         "ReqdColltnDt",
148:         &Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string(),
149:     )?;
150: 
151:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("Cdtr")))?;
152:     write_element(&mut writer, "Nm", creditor_name)?;
153:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("Cdtr")))?;
154: 
155:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("CdtrAcct")))?;
156:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("Id")))?;
157:     write_element(&mut writer, "IBAN", creditor_iban)?;
158:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("Id")))?;
159:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("CdtrAcct")))?;
160: 
161:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("CdtrAgt")))?;
162:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("FinInstnId")))?;
163:     write_element(&mut writer, "BIC", creditor_bic)?;
164:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("FinInstnId")))?;
165:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("CdtrAgt")))?;
166: 
167:     write_element(&mut writer, "ChrgBr", "SLEV")?;
168: 
169:     for (sub, bic) in bookings {
170:         let price = sub.total_price(event);
171:         let mandate_ref = format!("SEPA-{}", sub.payment_id);
172:         let sign_date = sub.created.format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string();
173: 
174:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("DrctDbtTxInf")))?;
175: 
176:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("PmtId")))?;
177:         write_element(&mut writer, "EndToEndId", &mandate_ref)?;
178:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("PmtId")))?;
179: 
180:         write_element_with_attr(
181:             &mut writer,
182:             "InstdAmt",
183:             ("Ccy", "EUR"),
184:             &format!("{:.2}", price),
185:         )?;
186: 
187:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("DrctDbtTx")))?;
188:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("MndtRltdInf")))?;
189:         write_element(&mut writer, "MndtId", &mandate_ref)?;
190:         write_element(&mut writer, "DtOfSgntr", &sign_date)?;
191:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("MndtRltdInf")))?;
192:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("DrctDbtTx")))?;
193: 
194:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("DbtrAgt")))?;
195:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("FinInstnId")))?;
196:         write_element(&mut writer, "BIC", bic)?;
197:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("FinInstnId")))?;
198:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("DbtrAgt")))?;
199: 
200:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("Dbtr")))?;
201:         write_element(
202:             &mut writer,
203:             "Nm",
204:             &format!("{} {}", sub.first_name, sub.last_name),
205:         )?;
206:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("Dbtr")))?;
207: 
208:         let iban = sub.iban.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
209:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("DbtrAcct")))?;
210:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("Id")))?;
211:         write_element(&mut writer, "IBAN", iban)?;
212:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("Id")))?;
213:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("DbtrAcct")))?;
214: 
215:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("RmtInf")))?;
216:         write_element(
217:             &mut writer,
218:             "Ustrd",
219:             &format!("Teilnahmegebühr {}", event.name),
220:         )?;
221:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("RmtInf")))?;
222: 
223:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("DrctDbtTxInf")))?;
224:     }
225: 
226:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("PmtInf")))?;
227:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("CstmrDrctDbtInitn")))?;
228:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("Document")))?;
229: 
230:     Ok(String::from_utf8(writer.into_inner())?)
231: }

parts.text:
Now I have a complete understanding. Let me analyze the differences and present a plan to the user.

## Side-by-side comparison

### Working file structure (pain.008.001.02) vs broken file:

| Element | Working | Broken (our code) | Issue |
|---------|---------|-------------------|-------|
| `<Document>` | Has `xsi:schemaLocation` attribute | **Missing** `xsi:schemaLocation` | Bank software may require it (some banks do strictly enforce) |
| `<CreDtTm>` | `2026-04-28T18:35:13Z` (with Z) | `2026-07-11T11:37:56` (no Z) | **No timezone designator**. Spec requires UTC offset or Z. May cause bank rejection. |
| `<BtchBookg>` | `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>` present | **Missing** | This is optional in spec, but bank software may default incorrectly |
| `<PmtTpInf>/<SeqTp>` | `RCUR` (recurring) | `FRST` (first) | Logic missing - always writes FRST |
| `<PmtTpInf>/<CtgyPurp>` | `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>` | **Missing** | Optional, but bank may want it |
| `<CdtrAcct>/<Ccy>` | `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` present | **Missing** | **Required by XSD** for CdtrAcct type. |
| `<CdtrSchmeId>` | Present with DE54ZZZ00000299406 | **Missing** | **Required** for SEPA CORE direct debits by spec - creditor identification. |
| `<MndtRltdInf>/<AmdmntInd>` | `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>` | **Missing** | Required when there are amendment details (false means "no amendment"). Not strictly required when no amendment info follows. |
| `<DbtrAgt>` | Often uses `NOTPROVIDED` or `Othr` if unknown | Uses looked-up BIC | Working file does also use real BIC, so this should be fine if lookup succeeds |

### Now let me verify my understanding by checking the XSD for the specific required elements:

1. **CdtrAcct Ccy** - in pain.008.001.02 the CdtrAcct is `CashAccount16` which has `Ccy` as optional. But IBAN-bearing accounts usually need Ccy. Let me check the XSD.

2. **CdtrSchmeId** - definitely required in pain.008.001.02 CORE direct debits.

3. **CreDtTm timezone** - XSD allows UTC offset (Z) or no offset. Some bank software may require Z.

Let me check the XSD definitions more carefully:
Let me verify the XSD requirements for the missing elements:

parts.text:
Interesting findings from the XSD:

1. **Line 110**: `<xs:element maxOccurs="1" minOccurs="0" name="Ccy">` - Ccy in CdtrAcct is OPTIONAL according to XSD!
2. **Line 590**: `BtchBookg` is OPTIONAL
3. **Line 603**: `CdtrSchmeId` is OPTIONAL but REQUIRED by SEPA rulebook for CORE
4. **Line 617-618**: `SeqTp` and `CtgyPurp` are OPTIONAL in XSD
5. **Line 242-243**: `MndtRltdInf` and `CdtrSchmeId` are OPTIONAL in XSD

But the SEPA rulebook (not the XSD) makes some of these mandatory. Let me check the existing test:

Looking at line 343-354 of banking.rs - there is already an XSD validation test that passes! So the XSD allows the current output.

The issue is the SEPA rulebook requirements (different from the XSD). Banks validate against the SEPA rulebook, which is stricter.

Let me verify by also checking the existing test more carefully. The current code passes the XSD validation test. So the bug isn't in the XSD structure but in the SEPA rulebook compliance.

### Real differences and likely causes of rejection:

The most important SEPA rulebook differences:

1. **`<CreDtTm>` without Z/timezone** - The working file uses `2026-04-28T18:35:13Z`, ours uses `2026-07-11T11:37:56` (no Z). While the XSD allows this, most bank software wants explicit timezone.

2. **Missing `<CdtrSchmeId>`** - The working file has it with `DE54ZZZ00000299406` and `SchmeNm>Prtry>SEPA</Prtry>`. The SEPA CORE rulebook REQUIRES this. This is likely the main issue.

3. **Missing `<Ccy>` in `<CdtrAcct>`** - The working file has `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` in the creditor account. This is OPTIONAL per XSD but typically required by bank software.

4. **`<SeqTp>FRST</SeqTp>` is hardcoded** - The working file uses `RCUR` for recurring payments. This is a logic bug - we always emit FRST.

5. **Missing `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`** - The working file has it. May be required by some banks.

6. **Missing `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`** - The working file has it. Recommended for batch processing.

7. **Missing `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>`** in MndtRltdInf - The working file always has this. May not be required but bank software often expects it.

8. **No `xsi:schemaLocation` on Document** - The working file has it, ours doesn't. Some bank software may not require it but it's recommended.

9. **`<MsgId>` format** - The working file uses `1120325-0` (some meaningful ID), ours uses a UUID. UUIDs are valid per spec.

10. **`<PmtInfId>` format** - Similar, UUID vs formatted ID. Both valid.

### Most likely cause of bank rejection:

The single most likely issue is the missing `<CdtrSchmeId>`. This is **mandatory** per the SEPA CORE Direct Debit rulebook (SDD Core) - it's the creditor's SEPA identifier that uniquely identifies them. Banks will reject files without it.

Combined with:
- Missing `<Ccy>` in `<CdtrAcct>` - many banks require this
- Hardcoded `FRST` instead of configurable sequence type

Let me also look at the events module to see if there's any settings/config for creditor scheme ID:

parts.text:
I now have all the information I need. Let me prepare a clear plan for the user.

## Summary of differences

The broken SEPA file (ours) is missing several elements that the working bank-accepted file contains. Most are technically XSD-optional but required by the SEPA rulebook or expected by bank software.

### Critical issues (likely cause of rejection):

1. **Missing `<CdtrSchmeId>`** - This is the **most important** missing element. The SEPA CORE rulebook requires the creditor scheme identification. The working file has:
   ```xml
   <CdtrSchmeId>
     <Id>
       <PrvtId>
         <Othr>
           <Id>DE54ZZZ00000299406</Id>
           <SchmeNm>
             <Prtry>SEPA</Prtry>
           </SchmeNm>
         </Othr>
       </PrvtId>
     </Id>
   </CdtrSchmeId>
   ```
   The ID `DE54ZZZ00000299406` is the creditor's official SEPA Creditor Identifier (CI), assigned by the Bundesbank.

2. **Missing `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` in `<CdtrAcct>`** - The working file specifies currency on creditor account. XSD-optional but commonly required.

### Important issues:

3. **Hardcoded `<SeqTp>FRST</SeqTp>`** - The working file uses `RCUR` (Recurring). SEPA rulebook requires correct sequence type. FRST means "First" - for new mandates. RCUR means "Recurring" - for repeat collections. We don't know which one is needed.

4. **Missing `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`** - Optional in XSD but commonly required by bank software.

5. **Missing `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`** - Optional in XSD but recommended for batch processing.

6. **Missing `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>`** in MndtRltdInf - Optional, but bank software often expects it.

### Minor issues:

7. **`<CreDtTm>` lacks timezone** - The working file uses `Z` (UTC). Ours omits it. Some bank software requires timezone.

8. **`<Document>` missing `xsi:schemaLocation`** - The working file has it. Most validators don't require it but bank software may.

9. **`<MsgId>` and `<PmtInfId>` are UUIDs** - The working file uses a numeric/structured format. Both are valid per spec, but some banks prefer non-UUID.

## Plan

Let me present a comprehensive plan to the user.

# SEPA XML Format Analysis

## Comparison: working file vs. our generated file

The working file (`2026-07-17_CDC_...xml`) is from a bank/tooling that conforms to the SEPA CORE rulebook. Our generated file (`sepa-sepa-test-kurs-...xml`) has technical XSD-valid XML but is missing several elements that bank software commonly enforces.

### Critical differences (most likely cause of rejection)

| # | Element | Working file | Our file | XSD | SEPA rulebook |
|---|---------|--------------|----------|-----|----------------|
| 1 | `<CdtrSchmeId>` | **Present** (DE54ZZZ00000299406) | **Missing** | optional | **required** for CORE |
| 2 | `<CdtrAcct>/<Ccy>` | **`<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>`** | **Missing** | optional | recommended |
| 3 | `<SeqTp>` | `RCUR` (recurring) | hardcoded `FRST` (first) | optional | must match mandate type |
| 4 | `<CtgyPurp>` | `<Cd>OTHR</Cd>` | **Missing** | optional | recommended |
| 5 | `<BtchBookg>` | `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>` | **Missing** | optional | recommended |

### Minor / less likely to be the cause

| # | Element | Working file | Our file |
|---|---------|--------------|----------|
| 6 | `<CreDtTm>` | `2026-04-28T18:35:13Z` (with Z) | `2026-07-11T11:37:56` (no tz) |
| 7 | `<Document xsi:schemaLocation>` | present | missing |
| 8 | `<MndtRltdInf>/<AmdmntInd>` | `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>` | missing |
| 9 | `<MsgId>` / `<PmtInfId>` | `1120325-0` / `20260428-48346-339-1` (formatted) | UUIDs |

### Source locations

- XML generation: `src/logic/banking.rs:84-231` (`generate_sepa_xml`)
- Hardcoded FRST: `src/logic/banking.rs:142`
- Orchestration: `src/logic/events.rs:251-324` (`export_sepa_xml`)
- Secrets currently: only name + IBAN (`src/logic/secrets.rs:80-82`) — no creditor scheme ID stored
- Existing test: `src/logic/banking.rs:343-354` validates against XSD only (passes — but doesn't catch rulebook gaps)

## Recommended plan

1. **Add creditor scheme ID configuration**
   - Add a third secret `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` (e.g. `DE54ZZZ00000299406`) to `src/logic/secrets.rs` (`SepaCreditorId` variant, env key, getter)
   - Add it to `ConfigIncomplete` check in `export_sepa_xml`

2. **Write `<CdtrSchmeId>` block** in `generate_sepa_xml` after `<ChrgBr>` (and before the transaction loop). Use `Othr` + `SchmeNm/Prtry=SEPA` to mirror the working file.

3. **Write `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>`** inside `<CdtrAcct>` after the `<Id>` close.

4. **Make `<SeqTp>` configurable**
   - Add a `sepa_sequence_type` (or `sepa_type`) field to `Event` model + migration (e.g. `FRST` for first, `RCUR` for recurring, `FNAL` for last, `OOFF` for one-off) — or simpler: accept it as a parameter on the export endpoint
   - Pass it through `export_sepa_xml` → `generate_sepa_xml` and use it instead of hardcoded `FRST`

5. **Add `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`** after `<SeqTp>` in `PmtTpInf`. Could be a constant or a per-event config like the sequence type.

6. **Add `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`** right after `<PmtMtd>` in `PmtInf`.

7. **Fix `<CreDtTm>` formatting** — use `%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` (UTC) in `src/logic/banking.rs:113`.

8. **Add `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>`** inside `<MndtRltdInf>` (after `<DtOfSgntr>`) at `src/logic/banking.rs:190-191`.

9. **Add `xsi:schemaLocation`** to `<Document>` at `src/logic/banking.rs:95-98`.

10. **Extend tests** in `src/logic/banking.rs`:
    - Assert the new elements are present
    - Add a second test that verifies `<CdtrSchmeId>` content with the expected CI format
    - Keep the XSD validation test (it should still pass since all new fields are rulebook-required, not XSD-required)

## Open questions before implementation

parts.text:
Great, the user has answered my questions. Let me summarize the plan based on their choices:

1. **Creditor ID**: New env secret `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` - same pattern as existing SEPA_CREDITOR_NAME and SEPA_CREDITOR_IBAN
2. **SeqTp + CtgyPurp**: Hardcode `RCUR` + `OTHR`
3. **Polish items**: Include all (xsi:schemaLocation, BtchBookg, AmdmntInd, Z-suffix timestamp)

Now I need to present a final, focused plan. I should write this in the format the system prompt requires. The plan should be detailed enough to execute but concise.

Let me present a final concrete plan with the chosen options baked in.

# Final Plan: Fix SEPA XML Format

## Decisions locked in
- Creditor ID: new env secret `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` (alongside existing `SEPA_CREDITOR_NAME`, `SEPA_CREDITOR_IBAN`)
- `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` and `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>` hardcoded
- Include all polish: `xsi:schemaLocation`, `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`, `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>`, Z-suffix on `<CreDtTm>`

## Files to change

### 1. `src/logic/secrets.rs`
- Add `SepaCreditorId` to `SecretKey` enum
- Add `#[serde(default, rename = "SEPA_CREDITOR_ID")] sepa_creditor_id: String` to the secrets struct
- Add match arm `SecretKey::SepaCreditorId => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_id)`

### 2. `src/logic/banking.rs:84` — extend `generate_sepa_xml` signature
- Add `creditor_id: &str` parameter (the `DE..ZZZ...` value)
- Inside the function, in order:
  - Add `xsi:schemaLocation` attribute to `<Document>` (line 95-98)
  - Change `CreDtTm` format to `%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` (line 113)
  - Add `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>` after `<PmtMtd>` (after line 131)
  - Change `<SeqTp>FRST</SeqTp>` → `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` (line 142)
  - Add `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>` after `<SeqTp>` (after line 142)
  - Add `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` inside `<CdtrAcct>` after the closing `</Id>` (after line 158)
  - Add `<CdtrSchmeId>...</CdtrSchmeId>` block after `<ChrgBr>` (after line 167) with `PrvtId` → `Othr` → `Id=creditor_id` + `SchmeNm/Prtry=SEPA`
  - Add `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>` inside `<MndtRltdInf>` after `<DtOfSgntr>` (after line 190)

### 3. `src/logic/banking.rs:264` — update existing test
- Pass `creditor_id` to `generate_sepa_xml` in both test calls
- Add assertions for the new elements (`<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`, `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>`, `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`, `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>`, `<CdtrSchmeId>...DE..ZZZ...</CdtrSchmeId>`, `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>`, Z-suffix on `CreDtTm`, `xsi:schemaLocation` on `Document`)
- Keep the XSD validation block (still expected to pass)

### 4. `src/logic/events.rs:266-271` — plumb creditor_id and update incomplete-config check
- Read `secrets.get(SecretKey::SepaCreditorId)`
- Treat empty creditor_id the same as empty name/iban (extend `ConfigIncomplete` error)
- Pass `creditor_id` to `banking::generate_sepa_xml` at line 304

### 5. `.env` (local dev) and AWS parameter store (prod) — out of repo
- Add `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID=DE54ZZZ00000299406` (or whatever the actual assigned CI is) locally
- Same value to the secrets store consumed by the Lambda

## Open follow-up (out of scope for this fix)

- Membership-style events currently share the same export path as test/event-style bookings. The working file in the repo (`2026-07-17_CDC_...xml`) is clearly a membership run, while the test export is a one-off payment. Once this fix lands, decide whether `generate_sepa_xml` should branch by event type (FRST/RCUR, OTHR/OTHR, etc.) or move the SeqTp/CtgyPurp to a per-event column.
- The membership file uses `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` on the creditor account and has every transaction with an explicit BIC. The current code looks up the BIC per booking via `banking::lookup_bic` — if any booking BIC is missing, the whole export fails. Consider whether to fall back to `NOTPROVIDED` per the SEPA rulebook for known BICs.
- `<MsgId>` and `<PmtInfId>` are still UUIDs. The working file uses formatted IDs. SEPA accepts both; not changing unless you want it.

## Verification
- `cargo test` — both new banking tests pass, XSD validation still passes
- `cargo clippy --all-features`
- `cargo fmt`
- Re-export a SEPA file via the API, diff the structure against `~/Downloads/2026-07-17_CDC_*.xml`

Ready to switch out of plan mode and implement on your go.

1: //! Secret management abstraction for sve-backend.
2: //
3: // Provides a `SecretProvider` seam with two adapters:
4: // - `EnvSecretProvider` reads secrets from environment variables (local/dev).
5: // - `ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider` reads a consolidated secret from AWS
6: //   Secrets Manager, falling back to environment variables first so local dev
7: //   keeps working via `.env`. Caching is owned by the adapter, not a global.
8: 
9: use anyhow::{Result, anyhow};
10: use async_trait::async_trait;
11: use aws_config::{BehaviorVersion, Region};
12: use aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client;
13: use mini_moka::sync::Cache;
14: #[cfg(test)]
15: use mockall::automock;
16: use serde::Deserialize;
17: use std::{env, time::Duration};
18: 
19: const CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME: &str = "sve-backend";
20: 
21: /// Typed key for every secret the application reads.
22: /// Replaces raw `&str` dispatch so call sites and the provider
23: /// agree on the vocabulary at compile time.
24: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
25: pub(crate) enum SecretKey {
26:     DatabaseUrl,
27:     TaskApiKey,
28:     CaptchaSecret,
29:     GoogleCreds,
30:     EmailAccounts,
31:     SepaCreditorName,
32:     SepaCreditorIban,
33:     SessionSecret,
34: }
35: 
36: impl SecretKey {
37:     pub(crate) fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
38:         match self {
39:             Self::DatabaseUrl => "DATABASE_URL",
40:             Self::TaskApiKey => "TASK_API_KEY",
41:             Self::CaptchaSecret => "CAPTCHA_SECRET",
42:             Self::GoogleCreds => "GOOGLE_CREDS",
43:             Self::EmailAccounts => "EMAIL_ACCOUNTS",
44:             Self::SepaCreditorName => "SEPA_CREDITOR_NAME",
45:             Self::SepaCreditorIban => "SEPA_CREDITOR_IBAN",
46:             Self::SessionSecret => "SESSION_SECRET",
47:         }
48:     }
49: }
50: 
51: #[async_trait]
52: #[cfg_attr(test, automock)]
53: pub(crate) trait SecretProvider: Send + Sync {
54:     async fn get(&self, key: SecretKey) -> Result<String>;
55: }
56: 
57: /// Reads secrets from environment variables only.
58: pub(crate) struct EnvSecretProvider;
59: 
60: #[async_trait]
61: impl SecretProvider for EnvSecretProvider {
62:     async fn get(&self, key: SecretKey) -> Result<String> {
63:         env::var(key.as_str()).map_err(|_| anyhow!("Missing env var: {}", key.as_str()))
64:     }
65: }
66: 
67: #[derive(Deserialize, Clone)]
68: struct Secrets {
69:     #[serde(rename = "DATABASE_URL")]
70:     database_url: String,
71:     #[serde(rename = "TASK_API_KEY")]
72:     task_api_key: String,
73:     #[serde(rename = "CAPTCHA_SECRET")]
74:     captcha_secret: String,
75:     #[serde(rename = "GOOGLE_CREDS")]
76:     google_creds: String,
77:     #[serde(rename = "EMAIL_ACCOUNTS")]
78:     email_accounts: String,
79:     #[serde(default, rename = "SEPA_CREDITOR_NAME")]
80:     sepa_creditor_name: String,
81:     #[serde(default, rename = "SEPA_CREDITOR_IBAN")]
82:     sepa_creditor_iban: String,
83:     #[serde(rename = "SESSION_SECRET")]
84:     session_secret: String,
85: }
86: 
87: /// Reads the consolidated AWS secret, falling back to environment variables.
88: /// Owns its own cache and lazily-initialized AWS client.
89: pub(crate) struct ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider {
90:     env: EnvSecretProvider,
91:     cache: Cache<String, Secrets>,
92:     client: tokio::sync::OnceCell<Client>,
93: }
94: 
95: impl ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider {
96:     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
97:         Self {
98:             env: EnvSecretProvider,
99:             cache: Cache::builder()
100:                 .time_to_live(Duration::from_secs(900)) // 15 minutes
101:                 .build(),
102:             client: tokio::sync::OnceCell::new(),
103:         }
104:     }
105: 
106:     async fn client(&self) -> Result<&Client> {
107:         self.client
108:             .get_or_try_init(|| async {
109:                 let config = aws_config::defaults(BehaviorVersion::latest())
110:                     .region(Region::new(
111:                         env::var("AWS_REGION").unwrap_or_else(|_| "eu-central-1".to_string()),
112:                     ))
113:                     .load()
114:                     .await;
115:                 Ok::<Client, anyhow::Error>(Client::new(&config))
116:             })
117:             .await
118:     }
119: 
120:     async fn load_secrets(&self) -> Result<Secrets> {
121:         let secret_name = CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME.to_string();
122:         if let Some(secrets) = self.cache.get(&secret_name) {
123:             return Ok(secrets);
124:         }
125: 
126:         let client = self.client().await?;
127:         let resp = client
128:             .get_secret_value()
129:             .secret_id(CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME)
130:             .send()
131:             .await
132:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("AWS SecretsManager error: {e}"))?;
133: 
134:         let secret_string = resp
135:             .secret_string()
136:             .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Secret {} not found", CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME))?;
137: 
138:         let secrets: Secrets = serde_json::from_str(secret_string)
139:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to parse consolidated secret: {e}"))?;
140: 
141:         self.cache
142:             .insert(CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME.to_string(), secrets.clone());
143:         Ok(secrets)
144:     }
145: }
146: 
147: #[async_trait]
148: impl SecretProvider for ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider {
149:     async fn get(&self, key: SecretKey) -> Result<String> {
150:         if let Ok(val) = self.env.get(key).await {
151:             return Ok(val);
152:         }
153: 
154:         let secrets = self.load_secrets().await?;
155:         match key {
156:             SecretKey::DatabaseUrl => Ok(secrets.database_url),
157:             SecretKey::TaskApiKey => Ok(secrets.task_api_key),
158:             SecretKey::CaptchaSecret => Ok(secrets.captcha_secret),
159:             SecretKey::GoogleCreds => Ok(secrets.google_creds),
160:             SecretKey::EmailAccounts => Ok(secrets.email_accounts),
161:             SecretKey::SepaCreditorName => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_name),
162:             SecretKey::SepaCreditorIban => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_iban),
163:             SecretKey::SessionSecret => Ok(secrets.session_secret),
164:         }
165:     }
166: }
167: 
168: #[cfg(test)]
169: mod tests {
170:     use super::*;
171: 
172:     #[tokio::test]
173:     async fn env_secret_provider_returns_value_when_set() {
174:         let key = SecretKey::CaptchaSecret;
175:         unsafe {
176:             std::env::set_var(key.as_str(), "test-captcha-secret");
177:         }
178:         let provider = EnvSecretProvider;
179:         let result = provider.get(key).await;
180:         unsafe {
181:             std::env::remove_var(key.as_str());
182:         }
183:         assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), "test-captcha-secret");
184:     }
185: }

(End of file - total 185 lines)
</content>

Wait, the test with `xml.matches('Z')` is wrong - `String` doesn't have a `matches` method on str directly. Let me think. Actually `str::matches` returns an iterator. But `xml.matches('Z')` - no, that doesn't work for chars. You need `xml.matches("Z")` for a &str pattern. Let me check - actually `"Z".matches(...)` no.

Actually `str::matches<'a, P: Pattern<'a>>` - so a `char` works as a Pattern. `xml.matches('Z')` returns iterator. So `.count()` works. That should be fine. Wait, but the import? Let me think... actually `matches` on str takes `Pattern`. Both `&str` and `char` implement Pattern. So that works. But I should double-check the count - the test has `DE54ZZZ00000299406` which contains 3 Z's! And `Prtry>SEPA` no Z. Hmm.

Let me reconsider - the creditor ID `DE54ZZZ00000299406` has 3 Z's. The CreDtTm has 1 Z. So total Z count would be 4. My assertion `>= 1` is fine but not very specific. Let me simplify and just check that the CreDtTm element ends in Z. Better: check for `</CreDtTm>` containing... well, can't easily check substring of an element. Let me just simplify and check that the XML contains `T...Z</CreDtTm>` pattern - or simply that it contains a Z.

Actually let me simplify by removing the count assertion and just using `contains("Z</CreDtTm>")`. But the CreDtTm has the timestamp like `2026-...Z` followed by `</CreDtTm>`. So `Z</CreDtTm>` should be present.

parts.text:
The build fails because of SQLx macros require database access. This is happening because the build needs to verify SQL queries online. The user said: `cargo sqlx prepare -- --all-targets` to update offline metadata after query changes. But these errors are not from my changes - they're from existing queries trying to connect to a DB.

Wait, looking carefully, my changes didn't touch any `query!` macros. The build errors are in sqlx online query checking, but I didn't change any queries. The errors are pre-existing because the build can't connect to the database.

Let me check the AGENTS.md guidance - it says `cargo sqlx prepare -- --all-targets` updates SQLx offline metadata. Let me check if there's an `sqlx-data.json` or `.sqlx` directory that should be used foroffline mode.
Build fails due to SQLx macros requiring database connection — not related to my changes (I didn't touch any queries). Let me check if there's offline metadata to enable offline mode:

parts.text:
Done. SEPA XML now matches the working bank-accepted file structure.

## Changes
- `src/logic/secrets.rs` — new `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` secret (env key + struct field + getter)
- `src/logic/banking.rs:generate_sepa_xml` — added `creditor_id` param, plus:
  - `xsi:schemaLocation` on `<Document>`
  - `<CreDtTm>` Z-suffix (UTC)
  - `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`
  - `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` (was hardcoded `FRST`)
  - `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`
  - `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` in `<CdtrAcct>`
  - `<CdtrSchmeId>` block with `PrvtId/Othr/Id` + `SchmeNm/Prtry=SEPA` (the **main fix**)
  - `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>` in every `<MndtRltdInf>`
- `src/logic/events.rs:export_sepa_xml` — reads `SepaCreditorId`, extends `ConfigIncomplete` check, passes through to generator
- `.env.example` — `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID=` added
- Tests extended with assertions for all new elements; XSD validation still passes

## Verification
- `cargo build` ✓ (with `SQLX_OFFLINE=true`, since `DATABASE_URL` in `.env` points to unreachable host `pi`)
- `cargo test banking` ✓ 3/3 (includes XSD validation)
- `cargo test secrets` ✓ 1/1
- `cargo clippy --all-features` ✓ no issues
- `cargo fmt` ✓ no changes

## Action required from you
1. Set `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` in your local `.env` (the value from the working file is `DE54ZZZ00000299406` — that's the legal SEPA Creditor Identifier assigned by the Bundesbank, you should already have it)
2. Add `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` to the AWS consolidated secret in Secrets Manager (same value) so the Lambda can read it
3. Re-export a SEPA file via the API and confirm

Note: `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` is hardcoded. For genuine first-time mandates (`FRST`) or one-off (`OOFF`), this will be wrong — the follow-up is to make it per-event or per-booking as discussed in the plan's open follow-up.
